actionable_feedback: Add an explicit weight-sensitivity sweep: vary declared weights (severity/recency/exposure) and show resulting priority/classification changes. At minimum include +/-5% and, per verifier note, also include +/-15% perturbations (document both) and report any class flips.

Fully quantify O3's modeled impact in the markdown and CSV (show the numeric transformation applied to mitigated_drop or priority, assumptions used, and resulting priorities/margins). Remove the truncated text in quantization_triage.md so reviewers can audit O3 effect.

Replace or justify 'needs_review' assumptions: either provide the source rationale for using equal weights and metric mapping, or demonstrate that outcomes are robust across a reasonable weight range (via the sweep).

Remove the unsupported claim that the grader referenced a verifier-note about +/-15% unless that verifier text is added to artifacts; instead document both the actual +/-5% sensitivity shown and any additional +/-15% analysis you add per step #1.
Add an explicit tie-breaking rule (for equal priority scores) and document handling of division-by-zero or missing-max cases in the reproducible scoring logic.

Include a short validation step that reproduces CSV floating-point results or provide a script/notebook and a checksum/hash of final artifacts so a human verifier can re-run numeric checks for parity.

Minor issues prevent a perfect semantic score: missing explicit weight-sensitivity sweep (weights were assumed equal and flagged 'needs_review'), partially specified O3 impact (truncated description), and one unsupported grader claim referencing +/-15% perturbations not present in artifacts.
